differential_diagnoses: Nutritional optic neuropathy (Vitamin A deficiency, xerophthalmia)
next_steps: Measure serum vitamin A (retinol), Vitamin B12, copper, ceruloplasmin, and zinc levels
Consult ophthalmology for formal electroretinography (ERG) and thorough funduscopic assessment
Immediate initiation of high-dose oral vitamin A supplementation
Complete metabolic panel and nutritional screening including vitamin E and folate
Genetic testing for mitochondrial disorders and hereditary optic neuropathies
Dietary evaluation and counseling by a registered dietitian familiar with ARFID (Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder)
Immediate administration of therapeutic high-dose oral vitamin A
Measurement of serum retinol, vitamin C, vitamin B12, copper, ceruloplasmin, zinc, and vitamin D levels
Consultation with pediatric ophthalmology for formal assessment of xerophthalmia and ERG
Consultation with pediatric psychiatry for management of ARFID
Referral to registered dietitian for nutritional rehabilitation
Dermatologic examination for follicular hyperkeratosis and oral exam for gingival health
Inpatient coordination with social work for long-term vision support services
